title: Falafel

ingredients.title: Ingredients (Makes ~20 falafel balls)
items: Chickpeas (dry, not canned) – 250 g (1 ¼ cups), soaked overnight (12–18 hours)
Fresh cilantro (coriander leaves) – 1 cup, tightly packed
Fresh dill (optional, adds depth) – ½ cup
Baking soda – 1 tsp (activated in water right before frying)
Aleppo pepper or chili flakes – ½ tsp (optional for heat)
Flour or chickpea flour – 1–2 tbsp (only if mix is too wet)
Oil for frying – neutral (sunflower, peanut, or corn oil)

instruction.title: Preparation Steps
steps: Soak Chickpeas
Wash and soak dry chickpeas in plenty of cold water overnight.
Do not cook them – authentic falafel uses raw soaked chickpeas.
Prepare the Mixture
Drain chickpeas very well.
In a food processor, add:
Chickpeas, onion, garlic, parsley, cilantro, dill, and sesame seeds.
Pulse until mixture is fine and grainy (like couscous), not a paste.
Add cumin, coriander, black pepper, Aleppo pepper, and salt.
Chill mixture in the fridge for at least 30 minutes (firms up texture).
Form Falafel Balls
Just before frying, dissolve 1 tsp baking soda in 2 tsp cold water and mix into falafel dough.
Form balls (35–40 g each, about golf-ball size) or patties using a falafel scoop or wet hands.
Fry
Heat oil to 170–180°C (340–355°F) in a deep fryer or deep pot.
Fry in small batches until deep golden brown and crisp (3–4 minutes).
Drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: Serving Suggestions
steps: Classic: Stuff in warm pita with lettuce, tomato, cucumber, and pickled turnips. Drizzle with tahini sauce.
Plated: Serve on a mezze platter with hummus, baba ghanoush, tabbouleh, olives, and fresh bread.
Modern touch: Garnish with microgreens, pomegranate seeds, or sumac onions for elevated presentation.

chefTips: Never use canned chickpeas – they make falafel mushy.
Chill the mixture before frying to avoid breaking apart.
Control oil temperature – too hot burns outside, too cold makes falafel greasy.
Double fry for extra crispness (short first fry, rest, then quick re-fry before serving).
Experiment with herbs – adding a touch of dill or mint can set your falafel apart.
